name: plugins
context: ESLint plugin ecosystem — typescript-eslint, react, import, and custom plugins in flat config format
info: ESLint plugins extend the linter with additional rules, processors, and configurations for specific frameworks, languages, and patterns. In flat config, plugins are JavaScript objects imported directly — not strings. This node covers the most important plugins (typescript-eslint, eslint-plugin-react, eslint-plugin-import, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y), how to configure them in flat config, how to create local/virtual plugins, and how to use shareable configs. Agents use this context when integrating framework-specific linting, understanding plugin rule namespaces, or creating custom rules.

sections.title: typescript-eslint
sections.body: The `typescript-eslint` package provides a parser, plugin, and configs for TypeScript. Use `tseslint.config()` to compose configs. Spread `tseslint.configs.recommended` for basic rules or `tseslint.configs.recommendedTypeChecked` for type-aware rules (requires `parserOptions.projectService: true`). Key rules include `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars`, `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any`, and `@typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises`.

sections.title: React plugins
sections.body: Install `eslint-plugin-react` for component rules and `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` for hooks rules (rules of hooks, exhaustive deps). Spread `reactHooks.configs.recommended.rules` for hooks rules. Disable `react/react-in-jsx-scope` for React 17+. Set `settings.react.version` to `"detect"`. Add `eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y` for accessibility linting.

sections.title: Import plugin
sections.body: The `eslint-plugin-import` provides rules for import/export: `import/order` enforces import ordering with groups and newlines, `import/no-duplicates` prevents duplicate imports, `import/no-cycle` detects circular dependencies. For TypeScript resolution, install `eslint-import-resolver-typescript` and set it in `settings["import/resolver"]`.

sections.title: Custom and local plugins
sections.body: Create custom rules as ESLint rule objects with `meta` (type, docs, fixable, schema) and `create(context)` function. Register as a local plugin in flat config: `plugins: { local: { rules: { "my-rule": myRule } } }`. Use AST node visitors in `create()` to traverse code. The `context.report()` method flags violations with optional `fix` functions.

sections.title: Prettier integration
sections.body: Use `eslint-config-prettier` to disable ESLint formatting rules that conflict with Prettier. Add it as the LAST config in the array so it overrides other configs. Do NOT use `eslint-plugin-prettier` (running Prettier as an ESLint rule) — it's slow and creates confusing error messages. Run Prettier and ESLint separately.
